name: Pickled Asparagus
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine water, white vinegar, and salt in a large pot. Bring to a boil and simmer for 15 minutes.
2. Remove garlic from the pickling spice and tie it loosely with cheesecloth or place in a tea infuser. Add to the boiling mixture.
3. Break asparagus ends off and blanch in boiling water for 1 to 1-1/2 minutes. Transfer to ice water to stop cooking.
4. Pack each jar with 1 garlic clove and 1 hot chili pepper.
5. Arrange asparagus spears standing upright in jars.
6. Pour the hot brine into jars, ensuring it covers contents by at least 1 inch. Seal tightly.
7. Store in a cool pantry for up to 2-1/2 to 3 months before opening.
Origin: A Taste of Oregon
Shared by: Sharon Stevens
